rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component type or implementation scope as appropriate
Preserve existing AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks at valid locations
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by inserting annex blocks in valid scopes
Preserve existing component types, implementations, features, and connections
Do not modify or remove existing AADL elements during annex insertion
Ensure inserted annex blocks are syntactically valid AGREE contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
anti_patterns: Avoid placing assumptions or guarantees in invalid scopes
Do not modify existing AADL architecture during annex insertion
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
